ABOUT SCOTT BUCKLEY

OMG hi welcome 2 my site!!!! This is the OFFICIAL fan page of SCOTT BUCKLEY, who is without question one of the most interesting people I know. Beer in hand. Axe nearby. Possibly on his way to Solvang.

Scott grew up in San Ramon, California where he bravely suited up for high school football at San Ramon Valley. He wore #55. He showed up. The scoreboard had other opinions. To be completely fair, suiting up and playing is 100% more than most people do, which is why we must honor his journey while also absolutely clowning on it.

After surviving high school football with his pride mostly intact, Scott headed to UCLA where he studied bioengineering, bitch. Not the easiest major. He also joined a fraternity and treated the beer supply like a senior thesis. At some point he worked at Norm's, which is either deeply impressive or extremely chaotic depending on how you order your eggs.

the man himself. doing bits.

There is a documented incident involving Napster. Scott allegedly got into legal trouble for music piracy in the Napster era. In his defense: everyone was doing it. In the prosecution's defense: not everyone got caught. This is the origin story of a man who would later study strategy professionally. The pipeline makes sense in retrospect.

Then -- AND THIS IS WHERE IT GETS WILD -- Scott got into HARVARD BUSINESS SCHOOL. Section H. Which, if you do not know what that is, Scott will explain it to you. He will find a way to work it in. He always does.

On the subject of beer: Scott once registered a 0.38 on a breathalyzer. For context, 0.08 is the legal limit. 0.30 is considered potentially fatal. Scott was talking. He has also peed his pants on multiple documented occasions. His wife, a woman of extraordinary patience and vision, addressed his bathroom accuracy issues by training him to pee in the shower. An elegant solution to an inelegant problem.

actual documentation of behavior

Off-season, Scott enjoys chopping wood with the energy of a man proving something to the universe, and making pilgrimages to Solvang, the Danish-American village that Scott has decided is his place. One time he fell into a rosebush. This is not directly related to Solvang but it feels thematically consistent.

he is proving something to the universe

He is also married which again: impressive. She is lovely. She trained him to use the shower. He would be lost without her in every measurable sense.

FANTASY LIFE HALL OF LEGENDS

🏆

HE DID IT. ONE CHAMPIONSHIP. IT HAPPENED.

Scott Buckley, armed with opinions nobody asked for, a frat house education in fluid dynamics, and an HBS degree he will mention, won a Fantasy Life championship. It was real. It was documented. It cannot be taken away from him no matter how many beers he drinks or rosebushes he falls into.


Scott has many, many words about his team. He will explain at length why his picks make sense. Sometimes, improbably, the picks do make sense. This is the paradox of Scott Buckley: a man who once blew a 0.38 on a breathalyzer and still had opinions, who fell into a rosebush and got back up, who peed his pants on multiple occasions and kept going. Champion material? Apparently yes.


"Told you." -- Scott Buckley, the one year it finally worked out

champion behavior. look at him go.
the whole squad. deep in Texas. legends all.

BUCKLEY TIMELINE

2000: Shirtless at Pacific Bell Park. No idea what awaits.


HS: San Ramon football. Wore #55. Showed up. Honorable.


UCLA: Bioengineering, bitch. Also beer. Also Norm's.


Napster: Got in legal trouble. Learned nothing. No regrets.


HBS: Section H. He earned it. He will tell you.


Rosebush: Fell into one. Got up. Kept going.


Marriage: She trained him to use the shower. A love story.


Fantasy Life: Entered. Lost many times. Then WON. 🏆
